creator: pav@FreeBSD.org
text:
State Changed
From-To: feedback->closed

Hallo 
At Fri, 14 Nov 2008 09:43:34 GMT, 
pav@FreeBSD.org wrote: 
>  
> State-Changed-When: Fri Nov 14 09:42:48 UTC 2008 
> State-Changed-Why:  
> So is there a problem with using TERM=xterm? 
no, there is a problem with using TERM=rxvt 
>  
> Is this problem specific to the FreeBSD port/package of tmux? 
Yes, at least it works fine on Linux.  
Obviously something to do with termcap. 
Today I tried 
cp /openbsd/etc/termcap $HOME/.termcap 
and tmux works fine. 

> If it is a general problem with the application, it should be reported to the 
> vendor, not here. 
>  
I ported some applications to *BSD and know quite well. 
But probably, I should have filed the bug to sys. 
> http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=128819 
Fulvio 

[ therefore I close this PR; if you get it fixed, please file a bug report ] 
[ against termcap ]
